ground_truth.title: Top 10 eLearning Companies Taking the Stage in 2025 and Beyond
ground_truth.author: Sofia Kondrat
ground_truth.main_content: During the COVID pandemic, e-learning cropped up as a means of enabling educational cohesion. In this context, eLearning compares educational practices such as online learning, virtual learning, distance education, mobile learning, and learning management systems. As we cooperate with educators, mentors, and businesses on this landscape, we observe a shift toward eLearning as the favored instructional method. There are currently 6,582 companies on LinkedIn with the keyword "eLearning," 28,230 with the keyword "e-learning," and a market size of $400 billion expected by 2026. The majority of these agencies are new to the scene. However, some industry titans are as close to household names as agencies can get. In this article, EducateMe looks at the top elearning companies in the US. We'll also explore the 6 biggest online learning platforms with the most users. Here are the top online learning companies to keep an eye on: eLearning management companies: EducateMe, Gurucan; Marketplaces for selling online courses: Maven, Teachable, and Udemy; Corporate training platforms: Docebo, TovutiLMS, AcademyOcean, and iSpring Learn; Online Education Companies: AffinitySpace, Guild. Companies that specialize in e-learning management provide robust online learning management systems (LMS) that include all the toolkits that students and educators might require. EducateMe stands out as one of the best online education companies for corporate training, encompassing employee onboarding, upskilling, extended enterprise, and customer training. With robust white-labeling capabilities and seamless integration via API and Zapier, EducateMe empowers users to swiftly set up and launch fully branded eLearning courses within hours. This eLearning company boasts powerful online learning automation tools, as well as engagement tools, live sessions, detailed data analytics, video conferencing, and networking features, all in one intuitive solution. Gurucan is a versatile eLearning platform designed to simplify the creation and management of online courses, memberships, and coaching programs. Tailored for entrepreneurs, educators, and businesses, Gurucan combines powerful features with user-friendly design to deliver exceptional online learning experiences. Elearning marketplaces have several advantages for customers and instructors. A marketplace gives instructors access to its existing customer base and a head start on marketing in exchange for a percentage of their sales. Students have easy and affordable access to high-quality educational resources. Maven is an elearning platform and cohort-based learning provider that empowers instructors to create and monetize cohort-based courses. Its primary services include accelerators, a marketplace for cohort courses, and community building. Maven provides an elearning prototype that can be implemented in a SaaS or Platforms setting. Teachable is a venture-backed e-learning company that provides instructors with the tools they need to create their own online classes and turn their expertise into a sellable product. The platform is currently used by over 100,000 different course developers. Udemy is an elearning company & marketplace that streamlines the enrollment and completion of e-learning courses, assisting professionals to pursue new careers, advance in existing careers, and enrich their lives. Udemy provides an international community for ingenious instructors to share their knowledge with a global audience using innovative tools and materials. Udemy's biggest selling point is its sheer size. The eLearning platform houses more than 200,000 courses on topics like design, development, writing, business management, networking, Cybersecurity, and more. Corporate training companies offer a space where organizations can develop training courses and tools for their employees. The goal of a corporate training program is to upskill or reskill the workforce to advance personal and professional development. Such programs take place in a one-on-one or group setting, and it's frequently instructor-led. Docebo learning management system offers a suite of AI-based tools for corporate education (employee training, compliance training, onboarding, etc.), and client & partner training. The employee e learning environment is best suited to medium-sized and large businesses. Tovuti learning management system is a comprehensive e-learning platform that empowers businesses and individuals to reach their full intellectual potential. The cloud-based solution equips users with the tools necessary to develop high-quality eLearning programs for the purpose of training their staff, partners, or customers. AcademyOcean is one of the best e-learning platforms because it provides enterprises with the tools necessary to create and deliver custom training for their employees, clients, and partners. Using this learning management system, educators, and entrepreneurs can create customizable courses, engage learners, and offer staff educational processes. AffinitySpace provides social and dynamic learning experiences tailored to modern workplaces. With a focus on collaboration, self-awareness, and real-world application, their neuroscience-backed content ensures effective and engaging training. Guild is an innovative platform dedicated to employee skilling and career development, providing comprehensive educational opportunities tailored to working adults. By connecting employees with high-quality online learning programs and removing financial barriers, Guild fosters equitable access to online education and supports career growth. Aside from Udemy and Teachable, these elearning companies have the most traffic in the industry. However, this doesn't make them the best online learning platforms overall. There's no denying that e-learning is on the rise, which means that more e-learning institutions will likely spring up at an alarming rate. Those who are serious about making a name for themselves and playing a significant role in the future of education technology will need to develop a universal platform that can accommodate all types of users.
with: During the COVID pandemic, e-learning cropped up as a means of enabling educational cohesion.
There are currently 6,582 companies on LinkedIn with the keyword "eLearning," 28,230 with the keyword "e-learning," and a market size of $400 billion expected by 2026.
EducateMe stands out as one of the best online education companies for corporate training, encompassing employee onboarding, upskilling, extended enterprise, and customer training.

Udemy is an elearning company & marketplace that streamlines the enrollment and completion of e-learning courses, assisting professionals to pursue new careers, advance in existing careers, and enrich their lives.
There's no denying that e-learning is on the rise, which means that more e-learning institutions will likely spring up at an alarming rate.
